mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units starts above 1400k signals a surge in demand for housing, which compresses mortgage rates and boosts equity prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1400k are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 5.5% signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE < 5.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 105 signals a surge in supply chain capacity, which boosts productivity and compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO > 105 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity prices over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 6200k signals a surge in demand for consumer goods, which boosts equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 6200k are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in initial jobless claims below 200k signals a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 200k are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 105 signals a surge in supply chain capacity, which boosts productivity and compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO > 105 for 3 months and HOUST > 1400k for 3 months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO > 105 and HOUST > 1400k are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
